cash_pressure: not_applicable; this is quote-source quality review rather than cash review
leverage_risk: medium-to-high; SOXL is a leveraged historical-position remainder and should use executable quotes only
opposite_exposure_risk: low; no opposite SOXL/SOXS reopening is visible
weak_position_drag: medium; NVDA weakness increases risk for SOXL even if semiconductor theme remains structurally strong
legacy_position_risk: medium; SOXL remains a historical leveraged-profit risk valve
rule_compliance: requires_order_ticket_confirmation; holding-page, watchlist, after-hours, or external quotes are not enough for execution
redeployment_readiness: blocked_for_execution; no automatic execution should use an untagged SOXL quote
key_findings: price: holding page around 261.550; watchlist/external quote around 280.54
quote_source: Longbridge holding page versus watchlist/external quote cross-check
execution_validity: invalid for automatic execution until order-ticket bid/ask is confirmed
trigger_validity: trigger proximity can be monitored, but sell/close decision requires executable order-ticket quote
confidence_level: medium for conflict detection, low for execution price until order-ticket confirmation
conflict_notes: materially different SOXL values mean trigger rules must tag quote source and quote type before action
recommended_next_actions: Add Quote Source Reconciliation / Quote Type Tagging as a runtime requirement before any quote-driven execution.
Use executable order-ticket bid/ask only for SOXL action decisions.
If executable SOXL price is below 270, sell 2 shares.
If executable SOXL price is below 260, close remaining SOXL.
If SOXL rebounds strongly but NVDA remains weak, consider selling 2 shares to lock historical leveraged profit.
Do not record a SOXL execution without order-detail screenshot evidence.
